title: Ice Clouds Found on a Giant Alien Planet
summary: Scientists using the James Webb Space Telescope have discovered ice clouds on a big planet far away called Epsilon Indi Ab. This planet is like Jupiter but has clouds made of water ice, which is surprising because it was thought to have ammonia clouds. The telescope helped astronomers see the planet by blocking the bright light from its star. The planet is cold but warmer than Jupiter because it still has heat from when it formed. This discovery helps us learn more about planets outside our solar system and how they are different from what we expected. It is a step toward finding planets that might be like Earth in the future.

keywords.explanation: a big telescope in space that takes pictures of faraway planets
a giant planet like Jupiter found in a distant star system
clouds made of frozen water high in a planet's atmosphere
the biggest planet in our solar system, used as a comparison
a bright object in space that planets orbit around, like the Sun

questions.question: What is the goal of studying planets like this?

background_read: This article is about a discovery in space science. Astronomers use telescopes to look at planets outside our solar system, called exoplanets. The James Webb Space Telescope is a new tool that helps them see these planets better by taking detailed pictures. Planets like Jupiter are gas giants, and scientists study their atmospheres to understand how they form and change. Finding ice clouds on a distant planet is exciting because it shows that exoplanets can be different from what we thought, helping us learn more about the universe and possibly find planets that could support life in the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Elisabeth Matthews at the Max Planck Institute for Astronomy
WHAT: Discovered ice clouds on the giant planet Epsilon Indi Ab using the James Webb Space Telescope
WHERE: In a distant star system in the constellation Indus
WHY: To learn more about exoplanet atmospheres and help find Earth-like planets in the future

perspectives.description: This finding challenges old ideas about exoplanet atmospheres and shows how new technology can reveal surprises in space.
